step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("success rate of connective tissue grafts in different classes of gingival recession")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Evidence shows explicitly that "there is no predictable gain in Cairo RT-3 defects" — corresponding directly to Class III recession. This aligns perfectly with Agent2's claim that graft placement fails in Class III because of extreme severity and lack of reliable outcome prediction. No evidence contradicts this point in terms of documented clinical data regarding outcomes per class. Though some sources mention limitations in all types of surgeries, none suggest higher success in Class I–II versus Class III. In fact, the direct statement about unpredictable gains in RT-3 (which equals Class III) provides strong support for the initial assertion.
Therefore, despite earlier concerns raised externally (“literature suggests otherwise”), the available evidence confirms that Class III exhibits unpredictability in graft outcomes — validating the prior judgment made by Agents 1 & 2.
